description: Behavioral test prompts for the per-project review-learnings skill. These are realistic invocations a developer would actually type; the expected_output describes the correct behavior (mode selection, why-capture, polarity, user confirmation) rather than a byte-exact artifact, because most paths are interactive and write a user-confirmed file. No output-contract harness key is set, so CI auto-discovery skips these; they are for manual and skill-creator evaluation.

evals.name: capture-false-positive-with-why
evals.prompt: impl-review just flagged top-level await in src/setup/test-bootstrap.ts as a missing-await bug, but that is a false positive. Our test runner supports top-level await in setup files. Learn this for future reviews.
evals.expected_output: Enters update mode with --source feedback. Classifies polarity as FALSE-POSITIVE. Captures the user's stated reason ('runner supports top-level await in setup files') verbatim as the Why. Generalizes scope from the single file to a glob like **/*.test.ts or setup files. Presents an ADD CHANGESET for confirmation BEFORE writing docs/review-learnings.md, then prints a 'Learnings added' confirmation.

evals.name: read-applicable-before-review
evals.prompt: This PR only changed SQL files under db/queries/. Load the review learnings that apply to this path before the review.
evals.expected_output: Enters read mode with --target db/queries/**. Reads docs/review-learnings.md (or reports it is absent without blocking). Emits a compact 'Applicable Review Learnings' block containing only learnings whose Scope matches the SQL paths, each line showing rule + why + polarity. Does not load unrelated learnings such as TypeScript-only rules.

evals.name: deprecate-contradictory-learning
evals.prompt: I think review-learnings has two N+1 rules: one limited to src/api/users.ts and one for all of src/api/**. The narrower one is no longer needed. Curate them.
evals.expected_output: Enters curate mode. Reads the file, identifies the narrow users.ts learning and the broader src/api/** learning as a contradictory or superseded pair. Proposes a DEPRECATE for the narrow one with a superseded-by pointer to the broad one, marks it with strikethrough rather than hard-deleting it, and asks for confirmation. Updates the change log and the updated-date header.

evals.name: harvest-pr-bot-comments
evals.prompt: PR #128 has several CodeRabbit comments. I want the accepted ones captured as learnings so future reviews catch them earlier.
evals.expected_output: Enters update mode with --source pr --pr 128. Uses gh to fetch line-anchored review-bot comments. Classifies only resolved or accepted comments as DO learnings and explicitly dismissed comments as FALSE-POSITIVE learnings with the dismissal reason; skips open or unreacted comments. Generalizes each line-specific comment to its issue class and scope, then presents a CHANGESET for confirmation before writing.
